title: Business associate professionals n.e.c.
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate activities designed to create or maintain a favorable public image or raise issue awareness for their organization or client. Plan, direct, or coordinate activities to solicit and maintain funds for special projects or nonprofit organizations. Plan, direct, or coordinate one or more administrative services of an organization, such as records and information management, mail distribution, and other office support services. (Based on 115 related US occupations.)

work_activities.element_name: Communicating with Supervisors, Peers, or Subordinates
Evaluating Information to Determine Compliance with Standards
Communicating with People Outside the Organization
Interpreting the Meaning of Information for Others
Judging the Qualities of Objects, Services, or People
Resolving Conflicts and Negotiating with Others
Providing Consultation and Advice to Others
Estimating the Quantifiable Characteristics of Products, Events, or Information
Coordinating the Work and Activities of Others
Performing for or Working Directly with the Public
Operating Vehicles, Mechanized Devices, or Equipment
Drafting, Laying Out, and Specifying Technical Devices, Parts, and Equipment

work_context.element_name: Face-to-Face Discussions with Individuals and Within Teams
Work With or Contribute to a Work Group or Team
Deal With External Customers or the Public in General
Coordinate or Lead Others in Accomplishing Work Activities
Health and Safety of Other Workers
Work Outcomes and Results of Other Workers
Dealing With Unpleasant, Angry, or Discourteous People
Dealing with Violent or Physically Aggressive People
Outdoors, Exposed to All Weather Conditions
In an Open Vehicle or Operating Equipment
In an Enclosed Vehicle or Operate Enclosed Equipment
Exposed to Sounds, Noise Levels that are Distracting or Uncomfortable
Exposed to Very Hot or Cold Temperatures
Exposed to Extremely Bright or Inadequate Lighting Conditions
Exposed to Cramped Work Space, Awkward Positions
Exposed to Minor Burns, Cuts, Bites, or Stings
Spend Time Climbing Ladders, Scaffolds, or Poles
Spend Time Kneeling, Crouching, Stooping, or Crawling
Spend Time Keeping or Regaining Balance
Spend Time Using Your Hands to Handle, Control, or Feel Objects, Tools, or Controls
Spend Time Bending or Twisting Your Body
Wear Common Protective or Safety Equipment such as Safety Shoes, Glasses, Gloves, Hearing Protection, Hard Hats, or Life Jackets
Wear Specialized Protective or Safety Equipment such as Breathing Apparatus, Safety Harness, Full Protection Suits, or Radiation Protection
Impact of Decisions on Co-workers or Company Results
Importance of Being Exact or Accurate
Pace Determined by Speed of Equipment

tasks.task: Identify opportunities or develop plans for sustainability projects or programs to improve energy efficiency and minimize pollution or waste; advocate sustainability to community groups, government agencies, and special interest groups.
Prepare reports or recommendations on transportation planning, evaluating transportation project needs or costs, and assessing the effects of regulatory limitations on land use projects.
Compile and analyze information, research case law or previous case hearings, and prepare evidence for disciplinary hearings relating to labor relations activities.

Hold public meetings with officials and community members to formulate or address issues regarding land use or community plans; participate in public meetings or hearings to explain planning proposals.

Conduct field investigations, surveys, and impact studies to compile and analyze data on economic, social, and regulatory factors affecting land use, including evaluating proposals for infrastructure projects for environmental impact.
Assess the feasibility of land use proposals and recommend approval, denial, or conditional approval of proposals.
Negotiate contracts with various stakeholders for collaboration on land use projects, policy making, and community development initiatives.
Compile information on terminology and information to be used in translations, and ensure translation accuracy by conferring with authors and checking original texts.
Review orders and maintain records of business transactions and product inventories; report data to companies or government agencies as necessary.
Prepare or review engineering studies or specifications and design, promote, or administer government plans or policies affecting land use, zoning, public utilities, and community facilities.

Collaborate with other professionals to develop sustainable transportation strategies; negotiate prices, discount terms, or transportation arrangements with suppliers to achieve successful project implementation.
Monitor company or workforce adherence to labor agreements and write letters related to labor relations activities such as those to amend collective bargaining agreements.

Plan, direct, or coordinate activities designed to create or maintain a favorable public image or raise issue awareness for their organization or client, including preparing necessary documents for planned project approvals or permits.

Conduct interviews, surveys, and compile data for further analysis to inform the development of land use proposals or sustainability projects, discussing with clients and determining any fees for services provided.
Prepare, develop, and maintain project documentation including environmental documents, reports on land use data, or community facilities requirements.
Analyze customer needs or preferences through conferred discussions with sales or purchasing personnel to inform land development proposals or sustainability initiatives.

Keep informed about economic or legal issues involved in zoning codes, building codes, or environmental regulations to provide accurate advice to planning officials and stakeholders on project feasibility.
Evaluate individuals' abilities, needs, and physical conditions in the context of project proposals, adapting programs as needed to maximize positive outcomes for stakeholders involved.
Develop methods to monitor employee satisfaction with policies or working conditions, including grievance or complaint procedures relevant to collective bargaining or labor negotiations.

Conduct research or provide instruction on community planning and sustainable practices to inform stakeholders and participants, while advising on health and safety policies relevant to environmental engagement.

Prepare reports or presentations to communicate employee satisfaction or related data to management; prepare and submit required governmental reports or forms related to labor relations matters, such as equal employment opportunity (EEO) forms, new hire forms, or minority compensation reports.

Present the position of the company or of labor during arbitration or other labor negotiations; propose resolutions for collective bargaining or other labor or contract negotiations; recommend collective bargaining strategies, goals, or objectives; draft contract proposals or counter-proposals for collective bargaining or other labor negotiations; negotiate, renegotiate, and administer contracts with suppliers, vendors, and other representatives; interpret contractual agreements for employers and employees engaged in collective bargaining or other labor relations processes; draft rules or regulations to govern collective bargaining activities in collaboration with company, government, or employee representatives; call or meet with union, company, government, or other interested parties to discuss labor relations matters, such as contract negotiations or grievances; identify alternatives to proposals of unions, employees, companies, or government agencies; advise management on matters related to the administration of contracts or employee discipline or grievance procedures.

Review employer practices or employee data to ensure compliance with contracts on matters such as wages, hours, or conditions of employment; investigate and evaluate union complaints or arguments to determine viability; mediate discussions between employer and employee representatives in attempt to reconcile differences; analyze information gathered by investigation and report findings and recommendations.

Perform general administrative tasks, such as keeping records or writing reports; maintain and review computerized or manual records of purchased items, costs, deliveries, product performance, and inventories; assist in preparing and maintaining personnel records and handbooks; prepare reports to be submitted to company's data processing department; monitor and follow applicable laws and regulations; research employee benefit and health and safety practices, and recommend changes or modifications to existing policies; evaluate job positions, determining classification, exempt or non-exempt status, and salary; prepare occupational classifications, job descriptions, and salary scales; prepare reports, such as organization and flow charts and career path reports, to summarize job analysis and evaluation and compensation analysis information; develop, implement, administer, and evaluate personnel and labor relations programs, including performance appraisal, affirmative action, and employment equity programs.

Analyze price proposals, financial reports, and other data and information to determine reasonable prices; monitor changes affecting supply and demand, tracking market conditions, price trends, or futures markets.

Conduct regular reviews of property within jurisdictions to determine changes in property due to construction or demolition; investigate and assess damage to property and create or review property damage estimates.

Evaluate land and neighborhoods where properties are situated, considering locations and trends or impending changes that could influence future values; examine claims forms and other records to determine insurance coverage; report overpayments, underpayments, and other irregularities; resolve complex, severe exposure claims, using high service oriented file handling; collect and analyze relevant data to identify real estate market trends; monitor shipments to ensure that goods come in on time, and resolve problems related to undelivered goods.

Report overpayments, underpayments, and other irregularities; maintain claim files, such as records of settled claims and an inventory of claims requiring detailed analysis; review repair cost estimates with automobile repair shop to secure agreement on cost of repairs; evaluate practicality of repair as opposed to payment of market value of vehicle before accident.
Supervise claims adjusters to ensure that adjusters have followed proper methods.

Investigate, evaluate, and settle claims, applying technical knowledge and human relations skills to effect fair and prompt disposal of cases and to contribute to a reduced loss ratio; conduct detailed bill reviews to implement sound litigation management and expense control; interview or correspond with claimants, witnesses, police, physicians, or other relevant parties to determine claim settlement, denial, or review; determine salvage value on total-loss vehicle; adjust reserves or provide reserve recommendations to ensure that reserve activities are consistent with corporate policies; collect evidence to support contested claims in court; confer with legal counsel on claims requiring litigation; interview or correspond with agents and claimants to correct errors or omissions and to investigate questionable claims.

Prepare written reports that estimate property values, outline methods by which the estimations were made, and meet appraisal standards; write and submit appraisal reports for property, such as jewelry, art, antiques, collectibles, and equipment; compute final estimation of property values, taking into account such factors as depreciation, replacement costs, value comparisons of similar properties, and income potential; inspect properties, considering factors such as market value, location, and building or replacement costs to determine appraisal value.

Create and maintain a database of completed appraisals; maintain familiarity with aspects of local real estate markets; study sales records and inventory levels of current stock to develop strategic purchasing programs that facilitate employee access to supplies.
Prepare and communicate methods and techniques for selecting, promoting, compensating, evaluating, and training workers.
Attend meetings, trade shows, conferences, conventions, and seminars to network with people in other purchasing departments.
Review records for completeness, accuracy, and compliance with regulations.
Analyze employment-related data and prepare required reports, including advising management on organizing recruiting programs and providing information on job applicants.
Hire employees, conduct new employee orientations, and prepare or maintain employment records relating to hiring and termination events.
Address employee relations issues and interpret human resources policies and procedures.
Conduct surveys and collect data using methods such as interviews and questionnaires, and analyze that data to support various operational needs.
Collaborate with others to develop business methods and procedures, including office and file systems.
Review and analyze statistical information, monitor progress, and ensure data quality in research or surveys conducted.
Develop new methods and principles in mathematics or statistics to improve operational efficiency in various fields.
Mentor others on mathematical and analytical techniques and provide expertise in risk management or financial assessments.
Prepare reports, documentation, and proposals to support business operations and project initiatives.
Conduct research to gather information about organizational needs and employee qualifications, as well as analyze data sources for trends.

Communicate with and provide guidance for external vendors and service providers to ensure the organization, department, or work unit's business needs are met. Supervise administrative staff and provide training and orientation to new staff. Monitor employees' work schedules and attendance for payroll purposes. Represent work unit at meetings or conferences and serve as liaison for requests or complaints. Evaluate the performance of vendors, contract employees, or other service providers to ensure quality and cost-efficiency. Manage leasing of facility space.

Prepare reports or make presentations on internal investigations, losses, or violations of regulations, policies and procedures. Conduct audits of validation or performance qualification processes to ensure compliance with internal or regulatory requirements. Prepare and review operational reports and schedules to ensure accuracy and efficiency. Prepare reports summarizing information or trends related to manufacturing performance. Review financial statements, sales or activity reports, or other performance data to identify opportunities for cost reductions or service improvements. Prepare detailed reports or design statements, based on results of validation and qualification tests or reviews of procedures and protocols. Analyze validation test data to determine whether systems or processes have met validation criteria or to identify root causes of production problems. Monitor security policies, programs or procedures to ensure compliance with internal security policies, or applicable government security requirements, policies, and directives. Review health, safety, accident, or worker compensation records to evaluate safety program effectiveness or to identify jobs with high incidence of injury.

Conduct interviews or surveys of users or customers to collect information on topics, such as requirements, needs, fatigue, ergonomics, or interfaces. Study product characteristics or customer requirements to determine validation objectives and standards. Conduct market research and analyze industry trends. Attend meetings, professional seminars, or conferences to keep abreast of changes in executive legislative directives or new technologies impacting security operations. Develop, conduct, support, or assist in governmental reviews, internal corporate evaluations, or assessments of the overall effectiveness of facility and personnel security processes.

Plan, direct, or coordinate activities designed to create or maintain a favorable public image or raise issue awareness for their organization or client. Plan, direct, or coordinate one or more administrative services of an organization, such as records and information management, mail distribution, and other office support services. Plan and implement changes to service offerings to meet community needs or increase funeral home revenues. Schedule work hours for funeral home or contract employees. Coordinate administrative, support, repair, or maintenance services for funeral homes. Manage security-related supplies, equipment, or technology. Coordinate security operations or activities with public law enforcement, fire and other agencies.

Set marketing, sales, or other financial goals for funeral service establishments and monitor progress toward these goals. Develop budgets for security operations. Analyze and evaluate security operations to identify risks or opportunities for improvement through auditing, review, or assessment. Develop cost reduction strategies and savings plans. Set goals and deadlines for the department. Determine root causes of failures or recommend changes in designs, tolerances, or processing methods, using statistical procedures. Identify opportunities or implement changes to improve manufacturing processes or products or to reduce costs, using knowledge of fabrication processes, tooling and production equipment, assembly methods, quality control standards, or product design, materials and parts. Assess the user-interface or usability characteristics of products. Develop, manage, or evaluate policies and methods to protect personnel against harassment, threats, or violence.

Prepare validation or performance qualification protocols for new or modified manufacturing processes, systems, or equipment for production of pharmaceuticals, electronics, or other products. Direct validation activities, such as protocol creation or testing. Validate or characterize sustainable or environmentally friendly products, using electronic testing platforms. Incorporate new manufacturing methods or processes to improve existing operations. Conduct validation or qualification tests of new or existing processes, equipment, or software in accordance with internal protocols or external standards. Develop validation master plans, process flow diagrams, test cases, or standard operating procedures. Design or evaluate human work systems, using human factors engineering and ergonomic principles to optimize usability, cost, quality, safety, or performance. Communicate manufacturing capabilities, production schedules, or other information to facilitate production processes. Develop or implement human performance research, investigation, or analysis protocols. Explain goals, policies, or procedures to staff members.

Assist in training equipment operators or other staff on validation protocols and standard operating procedures. Explain goals, policies, or procedures to staff members. Conduct classes to teach procedures to staff. Train production personnel in new or existing methods. Train subordinate security professionals or other organization members in security rules and procedures.

Attend or make presentations at community events to promote funeral home services or build community relationships. Negotiate contracts for prearranged funeral services. Sell funeral services, products, or merchandise to clients. Resolve testing problems by modifying testing methods or revising test objectives and standards. Respond to customer complaints, legal inquiries, payment negotiations, or other post-service matters. Develop, implement, manage, or evaluate policies and methods to protect personnel against harassment, threats, or violence.
Conduct research to evaluate potential solutions related to changes in equipment design, procedures, manpower, personnel, or training.
Direct or coordinate the supportive services department of a business, agency, or organization and oversee administrative services such as records and information management and mail distribution.
Prepare reports that include the degree of risk involved in extending credit or lending money, financial data analyses, and market conditions.
Administer online purchasing systems and prepare purchase orders and requisitions for supplies and equipment.
Develop and implement purchasing and contract management instructions, policies, and procedures, and prepare bid awards requiring board approval.
Represent companies in negotiating contracts and formulating policies with suppliers, and resolve vendor grievances.
Analyze market and delivery systems to assess present and future material availability and analyze credit data and financial statements to determine profitability.
Review purchase order claims and contracts for conformance to company policies, and evaluate processes for efficiency improvements.
Interview and hire staff, train employees, prepare schedules, and evaluate their performance.
Prepare detailed and summary reports of activities and manage operational budgets.
Supervise and review work of assigned subordinates and ensure compliance with laws, regulations, and internal policies.
Drive operational processes for the procurement and distribution of materials, and maintain inventory records.
Conduct quality analyses and audits of data inputs and outputs to ensure accuracy and compliance.
Compile, organize, and produce reports, protocols, and documentation related to operations and compliance.
Monitor status of loans and accounts to ensure payments are up to date and analyze financial data for decision-making.
Develop technical specifications and communicate data management needs to staff, and provide training on procedures or software.
Evaluate and monitor financial integrity in banking institutions, including loan portfolios and investments.
Conduct classes to teach procedures to staff, and review, and analyze new laws or regulations to interpret their impacts.
Maintain awareness of new processes and technologies to improve operational efficiency and staff utilization.

Classify, record, and summarize numerical and financial data to compile and keep financial records, using journals and ledgers or computers. Operate calculators and computers to perform calculations and produce documents. Access computerized financial information to answer general questions related to specific accounts.

Calculate, prepare, and issue bills, invoices, account statements, and other financial statements according to established procedures. Prepare financial reports to monitor client or corporate finances and calculate costs for billings or commissions.

Compile statistical, financial, accounting, or auditing reports and tables pertaining to such matters as cash receipts, expenditures, accounts payable and receivable, and profits and losses. Analyze technology trends to identify markets for future product development or to improve sales of existing products.

Reconcile or note and report discrepancies found in records and maintain accurate records of transactions. Perform financial calculations, such as amounts due, interest charges, balances, discounts, equity, and principal.

Review existing reports, dashboards, and other tools to provide technical support and maintain or update business intelligence tools, systems, databases, and dashboards. Management of timely flow of business intelligence information to users and the creation of technical design documentation for reporting solutions.
Identify or monitor current and potential customers using business intelligence tools and communicate with customers, competitors, suppliers, or others to stay abreast of industry or business trends.
Specify and document requirements for business intelligence or information technology outputs, including reports and dashboards, and disseminate information regarding tools or metadata enhancements.
Conduct tests to ensure intelligence is consistent with defined needs, and collect business intelligence data from available sources.

Provide assistance with the preparation of project-related reports, ensuring accurate compilation of data in various formats such as tables and graphs summarizing research results. Compile statistics from source materials, such as production or sales records, and perform data entry as required for project completion.
Conduct internet-based and library research to gather relevant market information and trends, and present research findings to groups effectively.
Keep accurate records of all goods shipped, received, and stored while verifying adherence of documentation to regulatory requirements.
Prepare invoices or cost quotations for freight transportation and manage payments or arrangements for shipping fees, insurance, and related transactions.
Negotiate and arrange transport of goods with shipping or freight companies and inform clients of factors such as shipping options and regulatory matters affecting shipments.
Verify proper packaging and labeling of exported goods while preparing customs paperwork and ensuring compliance with regulations.
Organize and track market position and shipment statuses for goods in transit, providing updates as necessary to exporters and consignees.
Maintain current knowledge of relevant legislation and political situations that could affect business operations.
Keep records of goods dispatched or received and maintain accurate records for compliance and accountability.
Analyze shipping routes and consider environmental sustainability factors to recommend solutions for minimizing costs and environmental impacts.
Notify customers of freight or baggage arrival, arrange for delivery, and track the progress of shipments.
Assess economic, industrial, and corporate developments by analyzing information from various sources to inform business decisions.
Confer with personnel and clients to ensure successful implementation of systems, procedures, and project goals.
Monitor compliance with policies and environmental regulations, conducting environmental audits for logistics activities.
Conduct statistical and data analysis related to logistics operations, utilizing technology and software for efficiency improvements.
Develop logistic strategies, including comprehensive supply chain designs that minimize environmental impacts.
Gather and organize information on problems or procedures, and prepare reports to communicate findings and recommendations.
Develop and maintain client relationships while providing technical support for logistics-related activities.
Attend meetings and professional development opportunities to enhance skills and knowledge in business operations and logistics.
Identify cost-reduction opportunities and improvements in logistic processes to enhance overall business efficiency.
Develop and implement proposals, project management tools, and logistical support activities that improve operational processes while maintaining business relationships with customers.
Analyze and interpret logistics data, conduct logistics studies, and determine feasibility and support requirements for efficient operational processes in logistics and inventory management.
Collaborate with other departments and conduct assessments including design alternatives and changes to ensure customer requirements and service agreements are met.
Prepare and validate documentation, and collect or compile technical data necessary for project proposals and execution to ensure compliance with regulations and safety standards.
Direct and support team activities, including mentoring staff and monitoring performance to drive efficiency and improvement in logistics and project operations.
Maintain records and documentation of logistical activities and ensure adherence to policies and procedures for operational effectiveness.
Prepare presentations and training materials to communicate project plans, progress, and customer engagement strategies effectively.
[Supplemental] Monitor street or utility projects for compliance to traffic control permit conditions with respect to community standards and public safety.
[Supplemental] Prepare and implement educational materials and activities aimed at promoting development in skills and competencies for logistics personnel.
[Supplemental] Document and analyze instructional content and methodologies for course effectiveness and student engagement.
[Removed] Test selected products at specified stages in the production process for performance characteristics or adherence to specifications.
[Removed] Prepare drawings of proposed signal installations using drafting instruments or computer-automated drafting equipment or technology.
[Removed] Train new technicians or other personnel on forensic science techniques.
[Removed] Examine and analyze blood stain patterns at crime scenes.
[Removed] Interact with the public to answer traffic-related questions, respond to complaints or requests, or discuss traffic control ordinances, plans, policies, or procedures.

Prepare manuals and train workers in the use of new forms, reports, procedures, or equipment according to organizational policy. Review procedures such as distribution or inventory management to ensure maximum efficiency or minimum cost. Recommend improvements to existing or planned logistics processes. Provide ongoing analyses in areas such as transportation costs, parts procurement, back orders, or delivery processes. Prepare reports on logistics performance measures. Maintain logistics records in accordance with corporate policies. Manage systems to ensure that pricing structures adequately reflect logistics costing. Develop or maintain models for logistics uses, such as cost estimating or demand forecasting.

Analyze logistics data using methods such as data mining, data modeling, or cost or benefit analysis. Apply analytic methods or tools to understand, predict, or control logistics operations or processes. Interpret data on logistics elements such as supply chain management or transportation. Compute reporting metrics such as on-time delivery rates or inventory turns. Track product flow from origin to final delivery. Monitor inventory transactions at warehouse facilities to assess receiving, storage, shipping, or inventory integrity. Develop or maintain freight rate databases for supply chain departments to determine the most economical modes of transportation.

Collaborate with teachers and administrators in the development and evaluation of programs, and in the preparation of schedules for curriculum offerings. Prepare responses to correspondence containing routine inquiries, and prepare invoices, reports, memos, letters, and other documents using word processing or spreadsheet software. Attend meetings and record minutes. Prepare agendas and make arrangements for meetings. Maintain accurate and complete student records as required by laws and regulations. Conduct research, compile data, and prepare papers for consideration by executives.

Attend meetings, educational conferences, and training workshops, and serve on committees. Prepare and promote career-related programs and events such as job fairs and career workshops. Counsel individuals or groups to help them understand and overcome personal, social, or behavioral problems affecting their educational or vocational situations. Assess needs for assistance and refer clients to appropriate services. Instruct individuals in career development techniques such as resume writing and interview skills.

Greet visitors and determine whether they should be given access to specific individuals. Provide customer service, answering questions and explaining available services to ensure customer satisfaction. Conduct follow-up interviews with clients to determine if their needs have been met. Maintain and organize detailed office records using computers to enter, access, and retrieve data. Perform general office duties such as ordering supplies and maintaining records management systems.

Coordinate and manage administrative services of an organization such as records, departmental finances, and housekeeping to aid executives. Plan, direct, and participate in recruitment and enrollment activities. Supervise and train clerical staff and other employees by scheduling training and organizing materials. Contact service providers for logistical needs such as rates and schedules, and communicate with clients to determine material availability.

Prepare insurance claim forms or related documents, and review them for completeness. Investigate and correct errors upon customers' request based on their inquiries, and correspond with insured or agents to provide information on account status. Facilitate the payment and processing of claims, reviewing insurance policies to determine coverage and contacting insured personnel for additional information as needed.

Perform clerical tasks such as answering phones, distributing mail, and documenting transactions. Process and record new insurance policies and claims, reviewing them for accuracy. Answer customer questions and provide explanation of services. Collect and log information pertaining to client relationships, ensuring proper data management and confidentiality.
Interview customers to obtain personal and financial data needed for accounts, loans, or credit applications, and advise them regarding procedures for services such as loans and transactions.
Collect and record customer deposits and fees, issue receipts, and keep records of customers' accounts, charges, and payments using computers.
Compile and analyze credit information gathered by investigation, including checking applicants' references and credit records, and review loan applications for accuracy.
Prepare and type loan applications, closing documents, and necessary forms using computers, and present loan and repayment schedules to customers.
Establish and maintain effective working relationships with clients, government officials, and media representatives, and increase public awareness of organizational needs or activities.
Plan, direct, and coordinate fundraising activities, events, and strategies, and manage related budgets to maximize contributions.
Develop materials for submission to funding organizations, conduct research on potential donors or investors, and encourage contributions through effective strategies.
Evaluate and recommend improvements to existing organizational practices related to fundraising and public relations efforts, ensuring compatibility and effectiveness.
Contact representatives from various sectors to promote organizational causes, build relationships, and arrange for recovery of financial losses related to fraud.
Gather and maintain knowledge of trends and issues affecting fundraising and compliance, investigating or reporting on potential challenges related to client relations.
Train other personnel in processes related to financial data management, fraud detection, and customer service techniques.
Process correspondence, applications, and paperwork related to accounts, loans, and donations accurately and efficiently.
Obtain and serve legal documents as needed, and prepare evidence for presentations involving financial matters or client transactions.
Research and evaluate new technologies or methods for improving operational efficiency in fundraising and public relations contexts.
Follow up with customers regarding delinquent accounts and payment collections, maintaining accurate records of communications.

Assess cost effectiveness of products, projects, or services while tracking actual costs relative to bids as the project develops. Prepare estimates used by management for planning and scheduling work, and for selecting vendors or subcontractors. Prepare cost and expenditure statements and documentation throughout the project. Analyze financial data to detect irregularities and prepare findings and recommendations regarding fraud detection and prevention strategies.

Consult with clients, vendors, personnel, and other stakeholders to discuss estimates, resolve issues, and coordinate activities. Develop and maintain relationships with suppliers, contractors, and other business associates.

Lead and participate in fraud investigation teams, conducting interviews and gathering financial documents related to suspected fraud. Document all investigative activities and recommend actions in fraud cases. Prepare written reports of investigation findings and testify in court as needed. Advise businesses on improving fraud detection and compliance.

Create and maintain logs, records, or databases related to financial and fraudulent activity. Conduct field investigations and monitoring of projects to ensure compliance with plans, specifications, and safety standards.

Prepare promotional plans and sales presentations to sell new advertising programs and protect existing advertising. Consult with company officials, sales departments, and advertising agencies to develop promotional strategies and assess advertising media and formats. Identify new advertising markets and propose products to serve them.
Evaluate subcontractor bids for quality, cost, and reliability, and recommend appropriate sizes and formats for advertising.
Attend sales meetings and industry events to gather information, promote products, and expand networks. Support researchers or traders with analytical applications related to valuations and data.

Confer with financial engineers or analysts on trading strategies and system performance. Develop models to research new financial products and address analytical issues. Prepare requirements documentation for software developers when needed.

Conduct investigations to assess potential risks in business operations and identify opportunities for cost reduction. Analyze worker or production problems and suggest solutions, including improving methods or procedures. Collect historical cost data to estimate future costs.
Monitor developments in the fields of industrial technology, business, finance, and economic theory.
Prepare plans of action for investment, using financial analyses.
Produce reports or presentations that outline findings, explain risk positions, or recommend changes to stakeholders.
Provide statistical modeling advice to other departments and conduct statistical analyses to quantify risk, using relevant software or econometric models.
Recommend investments and investment timing, and ways to control or reduce risk to clients, companies, or organizations.
Review or draft risk disclosures for offer documents and track, measure, or report on aspects of market risk for traded issues.

Write grant applications or project proposals to secure funding for sustainability projects, and develop sustainability project goals, objectives, initiatives, or strategies in collaboration with other professionals.
Prepare, maintain, or update documentation and reports related to security procedures, system designs, and emergency management plans.
Recommend improvements in security systems or procedures and conduct security audits to identify vulnerabilities related to physical security or staff safety.
Review existing disaster recovery plans and conduct testing of documented strategies and plans.
Create or administer training materials on sustainability and security procedures, and provide technical or administrative support for relevant programs.
Monitor or track sustainability indicators such as waste generation and recycling, and assess or propose sustainability initiatives considering cost effectiveness.
Compile reports summarizing operational results and financial performance of sustainability initiatives, and create marketing or outreach media to communicate related issues.
Investigate products or business opportunities to identify areas for improvement or viability, using online or offline sources.
Conduct corporate intelligence collection or data analysis to identify security threats, and assess security measures for effectiveness.
Design strategic improvement initiatives to mitigate business risks caused by regulatory or other changes that affect operation.
Identify or procure needed resources to implement sustainability programs or projects, and create plans related to such projects.
Develop conceptual designs or specifications for projects related to security systems and sustainability, ensuring they meet applicable standards.
Create and manage accounts and orders to ensure effective operations, including budgeting and scheduling.
Prepare documentation for reports summarizing investigations or organizational compliance with environmental and sustainability regulations.
Develop emergency management plans, including budgets for disaster recovery and business continuity strategies.
Prepare or analyze financial reports for operational results, enhance security operations, and ensure compliance with applicable laws and codes.
Evaluate the effectiveness of sustainability initiatives and make necessary adjustments based on findings.

Direct and coordinate activities to ensure compliance with applicable health, safety, or hygiene standards and inspect work areas or operating equipment to ensure conformance to established standards in cleanliness and maintenance.

Hire, train, and evaluate personnel; assign work schedules according to requirements and ensure quality and timely delivery of service while monitoring employee performance and addressing issues as necessary.

Monitor operations, including sales staff performance, to ensure goals are met; resolve customer complaints regarding worker performance or services rendered and apply customer feedback to service improvement efforts.

Plan, direct, or coordinate activities designed to create or maintain a favorable organizational public image or raise issue awareness; communicate with company officials to develop methods and procedures to promote business.

Participate in the development or implementation of programs and services, including spa services, wellness programs, and fitness training for employees, while organizing facility schedules to maximize usage and efficiency.

Maintain required documentation efforts to ensure compliance with domestic and international regulations and standards, investigate product complaints, and prepare necessary submissions to regulatory agencies.

Utilize computer skills and software to manage records, databases, and communication efforts; prepare reports and maintain current knowledge of relevant regulations, including proposed and final rules.
Establish budgets and financial goals for programs or services, and develop staff service or retail goals to guide staff in achieving these objectives.

Develop regulatory strategies and implementation plans for the preparation and submission of new products, and ensure that regulatory compliance is maintained or enhanced by formulating and implementing related policies and procedures.

Communicate regulatory information to multiple departments and provide regulatory guidance regarding the design, development, evaluation, or marketing of products, ensuring proper interpretation and compliance with regulatory standards.

Monitor regulatory affairs trends, conduct environmental audits, and evaluate regulatory aspects related to environmental issues and policies, including ensuring alignment with corporate sustainability initiatives.

Prepare and submit regulatory agency applications, reports, and correspondence, and maintain records of compliance activities, including submission materials and responses to regulatory agencies regarding product information or issues.

Train staff in regulatory policies or procedures, and contribute to internal compliance investigations and audits, ensuring adherence to applicable standards and the development of corresponding reports or documentation.

Develop and implement marketing strategies and campaigns to promote products or services, and manage the preparation and execution of budgets related to these activities and other operational planning.
Recruit, interview, and hire employees, and manage or supervise staff and contractors to ensure quality of work, adherence to policies, and proper training in compliance and operational issues.
Maintain client databases, records of decisions, and operational oversight to track compliance, performance data, and other records relevant to organizational activities and objectives.

Prepare for and coordinate various wellness or fitness events, including managing the scheduling of staff and facilities, and evaluating programs to ensure their effectiveness and compliance with standards.
Conduct needs assessments, evaluations, or reviews concerning wellness programs and services, and communicate effectively with clients or the public about organizational offerings and initiatives.
Identify and apply for project funding opportunities, ensuring the alignment of requested resources with organizational goals and initiatives.

Serve as a confidential point of contact for employees, maintaining communication channels and documentation related to compliance issues and responses regarding organizational procedures or complaints.

Monitor and review compliance systems and operational standards to ensure their effectiveness and adherence to established policies, while disseminating relevant compliance information within the organization.
Prepare management reports regarding compliance operations and progress and report violations of compliance or regulatory standards to duly authorized enforcement agencies as appropriate.
Provide assistance to internal or external auditors in compliance reviews and provide employee training on compliance related topics, policies, or procedures.
Provide technical support to wind field customers, employees, or subcontractors.
Oversee internal reporting systems, such as corporate compliance hotlines.
Plan programs of events or schedules of activities, and write and present strategies for recreational facility programming using customer or employee data.
Conduct quantitative risk assessments for human health, environmental, or other risks, and identify environmental contamination sources.
Estimate costs for environmental cleanup and remediation of land redevelopment projects and conduct feasibility or cost-benefit studies for environmental remediation projects.

Design or implement plans for surface or ground water remediation, and design or implement measures to improve the water, air, and soil quality of military test sites, abandoned mine land, or other contaminated sites.

Coordinate the disposal of hazardous waste and coordinate on-site activities for environmental cleanup or remediation projects to ensure compliance with environmental laws, standards, regulations, or other requirements.

Develop or implement plans for the sustainable regeneration of brownfield sites and implement plans for revegetation of brownfield sites to ensure regeneration of a wider area by providing environmental protection or economic and social benefits.
